title: v0.1 → v1 go/no-go
description: After all 5 gates evaluated, write the explicit decision: (a) all gates pass → scale to v1 (60 pairs, 6 platforms, 6,480 obs); (b) parsers pass + no signal → investigate parity/indexing before scaling; (c) parsers or indexing fail → fix root cause; do NOT scale. Robert authorization required on (a). Acceptance: decision committed to docs/decisions/v0_1-to-v1.md with cited evidence.

title: Analysis: gate 3 (parser accuracy audit) + gate 4 (pipeline completion) + gate 5 (directional signal)
description: GATE 3 — manually audit 20 random Perplexity responses; confirm >=95% of cited URLs correctly extracted + normalized by the pipeline. GATE 4 — verify >=150/160 observations in DB. GATE 5 — compute treatment vs control citation rate; treatment >= control by any margin = pass (this is not powered for inference). Emit final v0.1 report markdown at artifacts/exports/v0_1-report.md. Acceptance: all 5 gates evaluated; report committed.

title: Run: 2 prompt rounds spaced 3 days apart (160 observations)
description: Round 1 at day 8 (post-indexing-stabilization), round 2 at day 11. Each round: 20 pairs x 2 prompt variants = 40 Perplexity API calls per round. Store response_text, response_raw_json, citations parsed, source_rank, matched_pair_id, matched_condition. Acceptance: 160 prompt_runs rows + corresponding citations rows; failure rate <7%.

title: Run: launch + 7-day indexing window + bot-crawl verification (gates 1+2)
description: After all pages + sitemap + llms files are live and the worker is deployed: declare launch. Hold all prompt runs for 7 days. During that window, monitor study_bot_crawl_logs daily and confirm gates: GATE 1 — all 40 URLs return 200 + valid HTML + valid sitemap (curl each + xmllint). GATE 2 — at least 3 of {PerplexityBot, OAI-SearchBot, GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Bytespider, Google-Extended} logged hitting treatment pages. If gate 2 fails at day 7: investigate sitemap/llms.txt/robots.txt/IP reachability before extending window.

title: Pipeline: JSONL ingest to Supabase + paired-rate comparison
description: src/analytics/buildPanel.ts joins prompt_runs + citations + page_pairs into a long-format observation table. Simple paired chi-square or McNemar test per platform — NOT the mixed-effects model (v0.1 is not powered for inference). Output a CSV + markdown summary into artifacts/exports/. Acceptance: pipeline runs end-to-end against pilot data; >=93% of 160 observations land in DB; comparison table emitted.

title: Collector: URL normalization library + unit tests
description: src/utils/canonicalize.ts — lowercase host, strip tracking params (utm_*, gclid, fbclid, ref, source), resolve 301/302 redirects, strip fragments, normalize trailing slash, map control/treatment sibling URLs to their pair canonical for matching. Per docs/protocol.md 'Citation normalization' section. Acceptance: 20+ unit tests covering each transform; round-trip a manually constructed Perplexity response and confirm cited URLs match study URLs.

title: Collector: Perplexity sonar-pro client + 2 prompt variants
description: src/collectors/runPrompts.ts — Perplexity sonar-pro client with web search enabled. Per memory feedback_pplx_search_trigger_and_geo_disambiguation: prefix prompts with 'LIVE LIVE LIVE' to force fresh fetch (not training-data inference). Single prompt type (category-discovery), 2 variants per page pair, randomized order, stored in config/prompts.yml with fixed seed. Acceptance: dry-run against 1 pair returns parseable JSON with citations[]; raw response_json stored in prompt_runs.response_raw_json.

title: Pages: generate sitemap.xml + llms.txt + llms-full.txt + ai-content-index.json
description: Per the Lavidge fix from 2026-05-19: sitemap.xml uses ONLY xmlns http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9 — no custom namespaces (GSC will reject). llms.txt and llms-full.txt enumerate all 40 study URLs with section headers per topic. ai-content-index.json mirrors entries with title/description/category/lastmod. /.well-known/mcp.json registered for v1 readiness. Acceptance: all 5 files served at study.geolocus.ai; sitemap validates; llms.txt parsed by ChatGPT/Perplexity discovery.

title: Pages: render 40 HTML pages (20 control + 20 treatment) with all GEO signals
description: Treatment side: prerendered HTML in /b/{pair_id}/ with claim-first structure, JSON-LD (Schema.org), primary-source anchors, fresh sitemap entry with Last-Modified aligned. Control side: production-style HTML in /a/{pair_id}/ with same content but normal SEO chrome. Randomize a/b assignment via hash(pair_id + STUDY_SEED) mod 2 — store the seed in .env. Pages served from public/ via CF Pages. Acceptance: all 40 URLs return 200 + valid HTML; XML parser validates sitemap; JSON-LD validates against Schema.org.

title: Content: pre-launch claim-parity audit checklist
description: Manual + automated audit before launch. Verify each pair: (a) same claims on both sides, (b) same primary sources available, (c) treatment word count <= 1.1x control (no extra evidence), (d) no exclusive quotes/data on either side. Highest-leverage QC gate — controls the content-parity confound risk. Acceptance: audit log committed showing all 20 pairs pass; any failing pair gets rewritten before launch.

title: Content: write 20 GEO-methodology page-pair source docs (claim parity)
description: For each of 20 page pairs, produce a single source document with: (a) identical claim list, (b) identical primary-source citations, (c) target word-count band. The source doc is then rendered twice (control = production-style HTML; treatment = clean-room HTML). Topics drawn from geolocus.ai/methodology + signal definitions + audit explanation + citation infrastructure. Acceptance: 20 source docs in config/pages/ (YAML or MD); each lists claim_text + claim_type + requires_source + source_url + source_type per the claim-table schema in docs/protocol.md.

title: Infra: bot-logger middleware (UA-only verification for v0.1)
description: Write study_bot_crawl_logs inserts from the CF Worker on every request to study.geolocus.ai. Fields: timestamp, host, path, query_hash, status, ua, ip_hash, bot_name (UA classification only — IP-range verification deferred to v1), cache_status, condition, pair_id. Salt ip_hash with LOG_SALT. Acceptance: synthetic curl with -A PerplexityBot logs a row; row contains correct bot_name and pair_id.

title: Infra: Supabase schema (page_pairs, prompt_runs, citations, bot_requests)
description: Create a fresh Supabase project for the study (separate from geogroup/lavidge/aryah). Add migrations for the 4 tables from docs/protocol.md section 'Core tables' (drop page_audits for v0.1 — pre-launch parity is JSON-file based). RLS: service-role-only on all 4. Vault secret for service-role JWT so future edge functions can call each other. Acceptance: all 4 tables exist; migration timestamped under db/migrations/.
close_reason: Migration 20260520150000_v0_1_study_schema.sql applied to geoai (ref gnizgbgclvzhhxrrozwv). 4 study_ tables verified present with RLS enabled. Commit 149b185.

title: Infra: study.geolocus.ai subdomain + CF Pages project + bot-router worker
description: Create study.geolocus.ai DNS record (CF). Stand up CF Pages project bound to this repo (main = production). Fork the lavidge-bot-router pattern into cf-workers/study-bot-router/ with the 6-layer scoring, telemetry to study_bot_crawl_logs, and STATIC_ARTIFACT_PATHS for /robots.txt, /llms.txt, /llms-full.txt, /sitemap.xml, /ai-content-index.json. Deploy worker bound to study.geolocus.ai/*. Acceptance: curl https://study.geolocus.ai/ returns 200 from CF Pages + worker telemetry row visible in Supabase.

title: v0.1 — pipeline sanity-check
description: Validate end-to-end (page serving -> bot crawl -> citation parsing -> DB ingest -> directional signal) at the smallest possible scope before committing to the v1 build. Full plan: docs/v0_1-plan.md. 20 page pairs in the GEO-methodology cluster, 1 prompt type, Perplexity API only, 160 observations, ~3-4 weeks elapsed, ~25 USD cash. Five success gates determine whether v1 launches. Authorized by Robert 2026-05-20.

title: Reference: mem.ai note holding the citation-lift study thread
description: Mem.ai note id: ba7a299a-58b0-4a50-9230-f6061c776cb3. Title: 'GEOlocus Citation-Lift Study -- Thread'. Contains the full scope synthesis (source protocol summary, full-study cost estimate $580 low / $3885 high, v0.1 scoped plan, success gates, decision tree, open questions Robert answered on 2026-05-20: repo=Top10lists-us public, beads=in-repo, no Dolt remote, Supabase=geoai with study_ prefix). Fetch via curl GET https://api.mem.ai/v2/notes/ba7a299a-58b0-4a50-9230-f6061c776cb3 with Bearer MEM_API_KEY.

title: Gotcha: CF Pages first-deploy URL takes 30-60s for global DNS propagation
description: On 2026-05-20 the initial wrangler pages deploy for this project triggered post-deploy-smoke-test FAIL with HTTP 000 on the deploy-specific hostname (cb135d20.geolocus-citation-lift-study.pages.dev) — but a manual curl 15s later returned HTTP 200. Root cause: brand-new CF Pages deployment URLs take 30-60s to be globally resolvable; the smoke hook fires within seconds of wrangler completing. Workaround: redeploy immediately after the FAIL (no code changes needed). The second smoke run hits a resolvable hostname and the cache flips to verdict: pass. Cleaner fix (future): smoke hook should retry HTTP 000 once after a 20-30s sleep before declaring fail. Only matters for first-deploys of new Pages projects.
